summary.intent: Fix a 404 error that Linux users encountered when selecting an Ollama model in the web UI, caused by hostname divergence and missing fallback logic.
summary.outcome: All 5 planned changes were implemented and tested: base URL alignment, native API fallback, JS error humanization, Rust 404 error type, and a follow-up PR review fix forwarding the Authorization header. PR #618 was created and two rounds of review comments were resolved.

repo: Ollama discovery uses localhost:11434 (KnownProvider in crates/provider-setup/src/lib.rs:910) but the probe was using 127.0.0.1:11434 (OPENAI_COMPAT_PROVIDERS in crates/providers/src/lib.rs:1397) — a subtle Linux IPv6 divergence bug.
normalize_ollama_api_base_url in lib.rs was private (fn) and had to be made pub(crate) so openai.rs could reuse it for the native fallback.
The project uses 'just' for build commands (just format-check, just lint, just test) and 'biome' for JS linting.
Clippy on the full workspace fails due to a CUDA crate that doesn't build on macOS; crate-specific clippy (--package) is needed to avoid false failures.
Pre-existing rustfmt violations exist across the codebase; checking only changed lines is necessary to distinguish new from pre-existing issues.
Greptile is configured as an automated PR reviewer and raised actionable findings during review.

code.finding: OPENAI_COMPAT_PROVIDERS Ollama entry had 127.0.0.1 instead of localhost, causing Linux IPv6 resolution mismatches vs. the KnownProvider definition.

probe_chat_completions() needed a 404-specific branch for ollama that falls back to POSTing {api_base}/api/show with the model name; other errors (connection refused, timeout) must not trigger the fallback.

humanizeProbeError() 404 guard must exempt messages containing 'ollama pull' to preserve the actionable hint from the native fallback error — a guard was added after the initial implementation missed this.
probe_ollama_native() initially omitted forwarding the Authorization header, which would break users with OLLAMA_API_KEY set — fixed after PR review.

workflow: Running crate-specific tests (cargo test --package) is faster and avoids noise from unrelated crates during iterative development.
Checking fmt diffs by file path helps distinguish pre-existing formatting violations from newly introduced ones.
When a mock HTTP test needs multiple routes (e.g., /v1/chat/completions and /api/show), an axum Router with multiple route handlers is the pattern used in this repo.
PR review bots (Greptile) can surface real security/correctness issues (missing auth header forwarding) worth treating as P2 fixes.

friction: Clippy on the full workspace fails on macOS due to a CUDA crate, requiring --package scoping to get useful results.
Pre-existing rustfmt violations throughout the codebase make format-check noisy; changes must be manually verified as not causing new violations.
No Dolt remote was configured on the worktree, so the initial 'pull from main' step silently no-oped.
open_items: The pre-existing rustfmt and clippy violations across the broader codebase were not addressed and remain as tech debt.
The native /api/show fallback probe does not yet have an integration/manual test verifying the full flow with a real Ollama instance.
It is unclear whether other OPENAI_COMPAT_PROVIDERS entries (non-Ollama) might have similar localhost vs 127.0.0.1 divergence issues on Linux.
